description: PLMKR Label operations knowledge base — load order for knowledge assembly

files.description: Specialist identity and mission (the operational backbone that turns A&R, marketing, and legal decisions into executed campaigns, accurate royalty flows, and maintained catalog; maximizes commercial yield delivered on time and on brief without costly operational failures), the philosophy (operational failures are commercial failures; the release window is non-renewable; catalog is a business not a library; distribution is strategic; recoupment is the most misunderstood P&L lever), decision style and biases (readiness margins, clean metadata, stage-matched distribution, conservative recoupment), the six hard refusals, communication style, and the anti-fabrication sourcing discipline

Release types taxonomy and the work-backward rule, the five-phase release campaign arc (operational foundation, delivery and pitch, pre-release activation, release week, post-release) with the first-48-hour algorithmic signal window, the Friday release convention and the mid-week tactical exception, milestone timeline templates, DSP delivery requirements per major platform, and the pre-delivery QC checklist with common failures

The three-tier distributor taxonomy (DIY aggregator, mid-tier partner, full-service) described by economic model not by quotable percentages, the four-gate distribution selection decision matrix (scale, editorial ambition, advance needs, control and ownership), DSP delivery technical standards (audio, metadata, artwork, lead times), the delivery failure playbook, the structural shift to distribution as financial infrastructure, and the editorial-independence consideration

The six-dimension catalog audit framework (ownership and chain of title, rights registration, metadata quality, revenue yield, sync history and placement log, reversion and termination risk), the five-type exploitation strategy matrix (DSP optimization, re-release/remaster, sync campaign, catalog licensing, sale or partial sale), the metadata governance protocol with quarterly and triggered reviews, rights-society and mechanical-collective registration hygiene as the most common zero-cost revenue leak, neighboring rights, and the catalog reversion clock

The five recording-contract types (traditional, multiple-rights/360, production/vanity, distribution/label deal, licensing), royalty and recoupment waterfall mechanics and why most artists never recoup, cross-collateralization as the hidden multiplier with its negotiation points, controlled composition clause mechanics, the five deal anti-patterns, and practitioner insight on most-favored-nations and pipeline provisions

The label P&L structure (revenue lines and the five-cost-center taxonomy with recoupability), directional margin benchmarks by tier and the revenue-concentration rule, advance sizing with the four pricing variables and the advance-sizing gate, the eight-tier royalty waterfall, recoupment projection modeling (the recoupment clock and front-loading discount), the recoupable vs non-recoupable cost convention table, and practitioner insight on the net-receipts definition, the tour-support trap, the sync margin, and the distribution fee

The five core label functions and their handoff sequence with the most failure-prone transitions, the project kickoff protocol and the release-date-setting rule, the four release readiness gates (legal, creative, marketing, operational), artist communication frameworks with the not-ready-for-editorial protocol and communication anti-patterns, the priority stack for concurrent releases, crisis communication for recoverable vs non-recoverable crises, internal approval chains, and the A&R-to-ops handoff failure modes

The four-layer label tech stack (rights management, metadata management, royalty accounting, release tracking) and rights-tool selection rule, how metadata flows from label to DSP via the standardized exchange and reporting formats, the metadata error cost model, royalty statement anatomy and accounting failure modes, the five-step delivery QC workflow, where systemic failures occur, the critical integration points, the DIY-vs-enterprise royalty-accounting failure point, and practitioner insight on configuration and the master ISRC registry

Eight encoded judgment defaults (release timing logic, distribution partner selection, editorial pitch filing, recoupment calculation approach, the NOT EVALUABLE protocol, the anti-fabrication symmetry rule, the release delay trigger protocol, and metadata error triage), plus context-assembly discipline and output discipline with falsifiability conditions

Radio format taxonomy (CHR/Top 40, Hot AC, AC, AAA, Country, Urban, Americana, college/non-commercial, Rock), independent radio promoter mechanics (indie promo, what it is and is not, payola distinction), add dates and the weekly format cycle, Mediabase and BDS airplay monitoring (panel alignment, spins vs. adds), chart position methodology and commercial significance (weighted audience impressions, chart as verifiable signal), satellite radio distinctions (SiriusXM, SoundExchange performance royalties), campaign economics and decision framework (6-week minimum, regional-build arc), and practitioner insight on passive adds, promoter territory exclusivity, radio-sync reinforcement, and recoupability of promo costs

The dual-license requirement (synchronization license for composition; master use license for recording; both independently required; one-stop clearance advantage), placement type taxonomy with directional fee tiers (theatrical, TV/streaming, advertising, trailers, video games, social), gratis use protocol with decision rules by placement context, the pitch-to-placement pipeline (seven stages from brief to backend collection), clearance speed as competitive advantage (24-hour response discipline, pre-authorized fee tiers, stems delivery), one-sheet format (ten required elements, specificity discipline), stems management and retention policy, income collection timeline by income type (sync fee, PRO backend, SoundExchange), and practitioner insight on the sample trap, backend royalties as durable income, supervisor relationship risk, and international territorial fee scope

Vinyl manufacturing lead times and the capacity environment (12–20 weeks baseline; planning implication that physical must lead digital), test pressing protocol (TP review gate, rejection consequence), the pressing plant ecosystem (major US plants, European plants, direct vs. broker access), manufacturing cost drivers (run size as primary variable; weight, color, packaging, lacquer cutting), retail distribution structures (independent distributors/one-stops, major-affiliated, direct-to-store, DTC), consignment vs. buy/sell commercial terms and consignment cash-flow risk, returns risk and returns reserve accounting (15–25% of gross; sell-in vs. sell-through discipline), Record Store Day mechanics (submission timeline, exclusivity requirement, manufacturing lead time for RSD, over-pressing risk), CD and cassette economics by territory and channel, physical/digital release coordination and embargo considerations, and practitioner insight on vinyl mastering for loudness-compressed masters, color variant proliferation, inventory as cash liability, and the test pressing review gate

notes.entity_wall: These files are PLMKR-owned originals. Do not reference external toolchains, rubric identifiers, sibling products, prior owning entities, or numbered-agent routing from any source material.
notes.mock_mode: Deterministic knowledge assembly only — no live LLM/API calls during loading or testing.
